---
title: "Tutorials"
description: "Worked examples using the EpiAware packages together."
---

## Get started

Two tutorials, one for each approach.
Start with whichever fits your question — they can be combined in a single analysis.

::: {.feature-grid}
::: {.feature-card}
### Compose distributions into event trees
Wire delays into a tree with `Sequential`, `Parallel`, `Resolve`, and `Compete`.
One object simulates a case and fits to observed ones.
[Start tutorial &rarr;](composing-distributions.qmd)
:::
::: {.feature-card}
### Assemble an infectious disease model
Build a joint model from a latent process, an infection mechanism, and observation layers.
Every part is swappable through the one `as_turing_model` interface.
[Start tutorial &rarr;](assembled-model.qmd)
:::
:::

## Further reading

- Each package's own documentation has worked examples — see the [Packages](/packages/index.qmd) page.
- [CensoredDistributions.jl](https://censoreddistributions.epiaware.org/) has the most complete set, including a full BDBV line-list analysis.
- The [Bayesian Workflow](https://avehtari.github.io/Bayesian-Workflow/) book gives the modelling approach these tools support.

Have a tutorial you'd like to see? Suggest it on the [forum](https://community.epinowcast.org/) or see [Contributing](/contributing.qmd).
